stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
Key technologies mentioned include transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on model deployment and architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in generative AI tasks.
The model is categorized under key AI/ML tags including 'llama', 'transformers', and 'safetensors'.

stories.title: Anthropic shuts down Fable, Mythos models following Trump admin directive
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic has ceased operation of its Fable and Mythos models.
The shutdown was reportedly due to a directive from the Trump administration's Commerce Department.
Concerns cited by the Commerce Department involve the potential for a Fable 5 'jailbreak' to pose a national security risk.

stories.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical 0-day vulnerability exists in PeopleSoft software, which is owned by Oracle.
The exploit allows attackers to steal gigabytes of sensitive data from affected organizations.
The vulnerability is described as being among the most critical in the software landscape.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its primary function in NLP applications.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in preview, signaling a push for optimized local AI experiences.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The key feature is running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, running locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Fox is buying Roku
stories.author: Dominic Preston
bullet_points: Fox is acquiring Roku in a $22 billion deal, signaling a major industry consolidation in streaming.
The merger will integrate Fox's TV networks and Tubi streamer with Roku's platform.
Fox content will receive increased promotion across Roku streamers and smart TVs post-acquisition.

stories.title: ClinHallu: A Benchmark for Diagnosing Stage-Wise Hallucinations in Medical MLLM Reasoning
stories.author: Lei Zhu
bullet_points: Introduces ClinHallu, a new benchmark specifically designed to diagnose stage-wise hallucinations in medical Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in current medical hallucination benchmarks by focusing on the *source* of errors, rather than just data collection.
Identifies varied hallucination origins, including visual misrecognition, incorrect medical knowledge recall, and flawed reasoning integration.

stories.title: When Good Verifiers Go Bad: Self-Improving VLMs Can Regress on New Tasks
stories.author: Jianzhe Lin
bullet_points: The paper investigates the reliability of self-improving Visual-Language Models (VLMs) that use a 'verifier' to guide self-improvement via DPO.
It challenges the common deployment assumption that a stronger verifier will always lead to a stronger student model.
The research demonstrates that verifier quality is highly task-specific, leading to potential performance regressions when the verifier is mismatched to the task.

stories.title: ClinHallu: A Benchmark for Diagnosing Stage-Wise Hallucinations in Medical MLLM Reasoning
stories.author: Lei Zhu
bullet_points: Introduces ClinHallu, a new benchmark designed to diagnose stage-wise hallucinations in medical Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in existing benchmarks by focusing on the *source* of hallucinations (e.g., visual misrecognition, knowledge recall, reasoning flaws) rather than just data collection.
Aims to enhance the trustworthiness and reliability of MLLMs for critical applications like clinical decision support.

stories.title: Show HN: Exploiting Slack's video embeds to achieve E2EE communication
stories.author: victorio
bullet_points: A vulnerability was discovered in Slack's video embedding feature.
The exploit allows for the achievement of End-to-End Encryption (E2EE) communication.
This represents a significant security flaw in a widely used enterprise communication platform.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements translate to higher quality model outputs.
The updates also result in faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
Integration with Ollama is built-in, simplifying the deployment of local LLMs.

stories.title: Roblox exec says ticking a box for age verification is ‘not enough anymore’
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Roblox is implementing new facial age estimation technology to improve age verification.
The company acknowledges that simple self-declaration of age is insufficient for safety.
The feature was tested with a group of children as part of a collaboration with NBC News.

stories.title: Under-16 social media ban announced by UK government
stories.author: Dominic Preston
bullet_points: The UK government is implementing a total social media ban for individuals under the age of 16.
This policy follows similar measures introduced by Australia.
The ban will be complemented by rules restricting online interactions with strangers in games and livestreaming.

stories.title: SpaceX is now a public company valued for its AI potential, so what comes next?
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX has transitioned to a publicly valued entity, shifting focus toward profitability.
The narrative highlights the increasing investor interest in SpaceX's Artificial Intelligence capabilities.
The article prompts discussion on the future strategic direction of the company following its valuation change.

stories.title: Controversial FISA spying law expires tonight. The spying will continue.
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA) is set to expire.
Despite the expiration, the underlying certification for the spying program remains active until March 2027.
The story highlights the continuation of surveillance activities despite the legislative deadline.

stories.title: Towards Direct Latent-Space Synthesis for Parallel Branches in LLM-Agent Workflows
stories.author: Pan Li
bullet_points: Current LLM-agent workflows struggle with parallel task exploration due to sequential text input.
Existing methods for merging parallel branches (e.g., concatenation) lose structural information and cause redundancy.
The research proposes 'Direct Latent-Space Synthesis' to better handle structured, parallel agent workflows.

stories.title: Giving AI a Headache: Acoustic Adversarial Attacks to Computer Vision Applications
stories.author: Michael Teti
bullet_points: Acoustic adversarial attacks can disrupt computer vision systems used in real-world applications like autonomous vehicles.
The attacks induce physical motion in cameras via acoustic vibrations, overwhelming internal stabilization mechanisms.
This induced motion causes artifacts in the video frames, leading to potential system failure or misinterpretation.

stories.title: Abstracting Cross-Domain Action Sequences into Interpretable Workflows
stories.author: Scott Counts
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of extracting meaningful insights from noisy, granular digital application usage logs.
It aims to move beyond existing deep learning methods that struggle with noise and cross-application generalization.
The proposed method focuses on abstracting complex, sequential user actions into interpretable workflows.

stories.title: Listening with Attention: Entropy-Guided Explainability for Transformer-Based Audio Models
stories.author: Agoritsa Polyzou
bullet_points: Addresses the interpretability challenge in highly accurate Transformer-based Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) models like Whisper.
Introduces LEAF-X, a novel, model-intrinsic Explainable AI (XAI) framework for ASR.
LEAF-X utilizes entropy-guided attention weighting and multi-layer attention rollout to provide faithful and temporally grounded explanations.

stories.title: From Self-Supervised Speech Models to Mixture-of-Experts for Robust Anti-Spoofing
stories.author: Mickael Rouvier
bullet_points: Addresses the growing challenge of anti-spoofing due to highly natural synthetic speech.
Proposes transforming a self-supervised speech representation model into a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture.
The MoE approach aims to significantly improve the generalization and robustness of spoofing detection systems against unseen synthesis methods.

stories.title: Gaze Heads: How VLMs Look at What They Describe
stories.author: David Bau
bullet_points: Researchers identified a specific internal mechanism in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) responsible for image description.
This mechanism, termed 'gaze heads,' consists of attention heads in the language backbone that track the image region being described.
The discovery was validated using a controlled testbed involving comic strips to map narrative order to spatial attention.

stories.title: Persona-Pruner: Sculpting Lightweight Models for Role-Playing
stories.author: Jongheon Jeong
bullet_points: Addresses the high computational cost of using large, generalist Language Models for specific persona-based role-playing.
Proposes 'Persona-Pruner' to reduce model size and computational overhead while maintaining persona fidelity.
Relevant to scaling AI applications, particularly in complex, multi-agent systems (e.g., virtual ecosystems).

stories.title: Gaze Heads: How VLMs Look at What They Describe
stories.author: David Bau
bullet_points: Researchers identified a specific internal mechanism in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) called 'gaze heads'.
These gaze heads are small attention heads in the language backbone that track the image region the model is currently describing.
The discovery was validated using comic strips as a controlled testbed to study narrative flow and attention.

stories.title: Persona-Pruner: Sculpting Lightweight Models for Role-Playing
stories.author: Jongheon Jeong
bullet_points: Addresses the computational inefficiency of using large, generalist Language Models for specific, role-playing personas.
Introduces 'Persona-Pruner,' a method designed to create lightweight models tailored for specific character roles.
Relevant for scaling AI applications, particularly in complex environments like interactive simulations or games with many NPCs.

stories.title: AdaSR: Adaptive Streaming Reasoning with Hierarchical Relative Policy Optimization
stories.author: Xiaoyu Shen
bullet_points: The paper introduces AdaSR, a new method for adaptive streaming reasoning.
It addresses the limitation of traditional large reasoning models which require complete input before processing.
AdaSR is designed for dynamic, real-world scenarios like audio and video streaming where information arrives continuously and partially.

stories.title: Flood and Harvest: The Provable Necessity of Trivia for Generating Valuable Mathematics via the Lens of Language Generation in the Limit
stories.author: Junbin Gao
bullet_points: The research addresses the gap between formally verifiable mathematics generated by AI and the actual value perceived by human mathematicians.
It models valuable mathematics generation as a nested language generation process, constrained by a formal verification system (proof checker).
The work suggests that 'trivia' (or external knowledge) is necessary for AI systems to generate mathematics that is truly valuable, not just formally correct.

stories.title: AgentSpec: Understanding Embodied Agent Scaffolds Through Controlled Composition
stories.author: Lianhui Qin
bullet_points: The paper addresses the complexity of modern LLM agents, which are built as composite, scaffolded systems (reasoning, memory, action, etc.).
A key problem identified is the difficulty in isolating and understanding the contribution of individual components within these tightly coupled agent pipelines.
AgentSpec is introduced as a modular specification framework designed to represent embodied agents in a way that allows for better analysis and comparison of different design choices.

stories.title: Towards Direct Latent-Space Synthesis for Parallel Branches in LLM-Agent Workflows
stories.author: Pan Li
bullet_points: LLM-Agent workflows often struggle because they process structured, parallel tasks through a sequential text interface.
Current methods of merging parallel agent branches (e.g., concatenation) lose structural information and cause redundant computation.
The research proposes 'Direct Latent-Space Synthesis' to better handle parallel branches within LLM-driven agent systems.

stories.title: Your AI agent doesn't have a memory. It has a transcript.
stories.author: diran_adeola
bullet_points: The article addresses a fundamental limitation in current AI agents: the difference between true memory and mere transcript logging.
It discusses the concept of building a deliberate 'memory layer' for AI agents.
The focus is on advanced agent design, specifically incorporating mechanisms for intentional forgetting.
